title: calamagrostis arundinacea var. sciuroides
intro: Calamagrostis arundinacea var. sciuroides, also known as purple meadow-grass, is a type of perennial grass native to Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, purple meadow-grass is often used to make infusions and decoctions for treating various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
